name: nonce_and_labels_v1
description: Determinism dataset covering global nonce ordering and HKDF seed label domains. Teaches the executor's u64 monotonic nonce counter (sequential ordering, gap detection, concurrent access, overflow behavior) and the six canonical seed domain labels (router, dropout, sampling, lora_trainer, gate_noise, executor) including what each controls, why isolation matters, consequences of wrong labels, string vs enum representation, and how to add new domains.

entries.notes: 400 examples: 200 covering global nonce ordering (sequential ordering 50, gap detection 50, concurrent access 50, overflow behavior 50) and 200 covering seed label domains (what each controls 60, why separate 40, wrong domain consequences 40, string vs enum 30, adding new domains 30)

provenance.review_notes: Covers both dimensions of adapterOS determinism: the u64 nonce counter that orders execution steps in the tick ledger, and the HKDF seed domain labels that isolate randomness streams for each subsystem. Examples are grounded in actual crate implementations (AtomicU64, ChaCha20Rng, derive_seed, TickLedgerEntry) and address practical correctness and security concerns.

evaluation_gates: Model correctly identifies nonce as a per-session u64 starting at 0
Model explains gap detection via windows(2) nonce continuity check
Model explains why AtomicU64 fetch_add guarantees uniqueness under concurrency
Model identifies overflow behavior and recommends high-water mark mitigation
Model lists all six domain labels and their controlled subsystems correctly
Model explains why domain isolation prevents cross-subsystem RNG contamination
Model identifies compile-time safety benefit of enum over string domain labels
Model describes correct procedure for adding a new seed domain
intent: Train the model to reason accurately about adapterOS's two core determinism primitives: the global nonce that provides total ordering of execution steps in the tick ledger, and the HKDF seed domain labels that cryptographically isolate randomness for each subsystem. The model should be able to explain, debug, and extend both systems.

nonce_sequential_ordering.description: What the nonce is, how it increments, its role in the tick ledger, replay ordering, and cross-host consistency

nonce_gap_detection.description: What constitutes a gap, how to detect it programmatically, what gaps indicate, interaction with Merkle chain

nonce_concurrent_access.description: How AtomicU64 guarantees monotonicity, memory ordering choices, behavior under contention, testing strategies

nonce_overflow_behavior.description: u64::MAX behavior, wrapping semantics in release builds, high-water mark pattern, estimated time to overflow

seed_domain_what_it_controls.description: What each of the six domain labels controls: router gate scoring, dropout masks, token sampling, training init/shuffle, gate noise, executor root

seed_domain_why_separate.description: Why cross-domain RNG contamination is harmful, how HKDF enforces isolation, debugging and A/B testing benefits

seed_domain_wrong_label.description: Runtime and operational consequences of using incorrect domain labels, detection difficulty, blast radius analysis

seed_domain_string_vs_enum.description: Compile-time safety of enum labels, how to define SeedDomain enum, FromStr, serde, exhaustive matching

seed_domain_adding_new.description: When and how to add a new domain, naming conventions, backward compatibility, testing requirements

seed_domains.lora_trainer: Training loop randomness — weight init and dataset shuffle
seed_domains.executor: General executor randomness and HKDF root seed
